title: Quadrilateral types
description: Identify quadrilaterals based on pictures or attributes. Quadrilaterals included are parallelograms, rhombuses, rectangles, and squares.

assessment_items.question_content:
All parallelograms have opposite sides that are equal in length and parallel.  

**Which of these quadrilaterals are parallelograms?**  
*The matching arrow labels indicate that two opposite sides are parallel.*

Quadrilateral | Parallelogram/Not parallelogram 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with sides that are equal in length and parellel.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/92a19122b13c7539f99f1eabbcf611e9.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with two right angles and one set of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d98f3cff761c1c29e203f049797ba9e.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6fe792a3ff5c09428cda12053c908a20.png) | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints:
A parallelogram is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with two pairs of parallel sides.

Look at the pair of parallel lines below:  


[[☃ image 1]]

Notice that the lines are always the same distance apart from each other at any point—no matter how far they are extended, they will never meet.

This shape has two pairs of parallel sides, so it is a parallelogram.

![A four-sided figure with 2 pairs of
parallel sides. A set of parallel lines are drawn along
the top and bottom sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cb1c96aac53376bc4790ec1d6f34be5.png)
![A four-sided figure with 2 pairs of
parallel sides. A set of parallel lines are drawn along
the left and right sides. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27d1ec49f8dd5fff28157eb5f02de598.png)

This shape has only one pair of parallel sides, so it is not a parallelogram.

![A four-sided figure with 1 pair of
parallel sides and two right angles. A set of parallel lines are drawn along
the left and right sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2ddf213e900138adee40fc1ff261df2.png)
![A four-sided figure with 1 pair of
parallel sides and two right angles. A set of lines drawn along
the top and bottom intersect.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/76aa0d486c08c2deb0f505adf6194018.png)

This shape has no parallel sides, so it is not a parallelogram.


![A four-sided figure with no
parallel sides. A set of lines drawn along
the left and right sides intersect.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1cf70e51155243559c25ed7c4fdec579.png)
![A four-sided figure with no
parallel sides. A set of lines drawn along
the left and right sides intersect.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23d2e75f3b2f3c4a3bdd166760959180.png)

Quadrilateral | Parallelogram/Not parallelogram
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with sides that are equal in length and parellel.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/92a19122b13c7539f99f1eabbcf611e9.png) | Parallelogram
![A quadrilateral with two right angles and one set of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2d98f3cff761c1c29e203f049797ba9e.png) | Not parallelogram
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6fe792a3ff5c09428cda12053c908a20.png) | Not parallelogram

assessment_items.question_content:
All parallelograms have opposite sides that are equal in length and parallel. 

**Which of these quadrilaterals are parallelograms?**  
*The matching arrow labels indicate that two opposite sides are parallel.*


Quadrilateral | Parallelogram/Not parallelogram 
:-: | :-:
![A four-sided figure with 4 right angles and 2 sets of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d94f272fe38178322433da284521926.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A four-sided figure with a set of 2 parallel bases, but not parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e400e8d5ebcf78a9d50f0495b1b6d369.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A four-sided figure with 2 acute angles and 2 obtuse angles and 2 sets of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec636a0b4202b5a87991045d9163f49b.png) | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints:
A parallelogram is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with two pairs of parallel sides.

Look at the pair of parallel lines below:  

[[☃ image 1]]

Notice that the lines are always the same distance apart from each other at any point—no matter how far they are extended, they will never meet.

These shapes have two pairs of parallel sides, so they are parallelograms.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

This shape has only one pair of parallel sides, so it is not a parallelogram.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Quadrilateral | Parallelogram/Not parallelogram 
:-: | :-:
![A four-sided figure with 4 right angles and 2 sets of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d94f272fe38178322433da284521926.png) | Parallelogram
![A four-sided figure with a set of 2 parallel bases, but not parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e400e8d5ebcf78a9d50f0495b1b6d369.png) | Not parallelogram
![A four-sided figure with 2 acute angles and 2 obtuse angles and 2 sets of parallel sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec636a0b4202b5a87991045d9163f49b.png) | Parallelogram

assessment_items.question_content:
All squares have four right angles and four sides that are equal in length.

**Which of these quadrilaterals are squares?**

Quadrilateral | Square/Not square 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7424e6feac5c6e526c64cb736ed32ac2.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c3dc43bf0167878846308a171c7f8e43.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2307d7484219585f4f687299d6185020.png)  | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints: A square is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four right angles and four sides of equal length.

These shapes have four right angles and four sides of equal length, so they are squares.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

This shape has four right angles but not four sides of equal length, so it is not a square.


[[☃ image 1]]

Quadrilateral | Square/Not square |
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7424e6feac5c6e526c64cb736ed32ac2.png) | Square
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c3dc43bf0167878846308a171c7f8e43.png) | Square
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2307d7484219585f4f687299d6185020.png)  | Not square

assessment_items.question_content:
All squares have four right angles and four sides that are equal in length.

**Which of these quadrilaterals are squares?**

Quadrilateral | Square/Not square 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4006a3393d1388fe9f3bf8028fdcb2db.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d999a4b9b555f28a412e0972f38dd14.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d93227cd0e5b41a6c2e4571b554366eb.png)  | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints: A square is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four right angles and four sides of equal length.

This shape has four right angles and four sides of equal length, so it is a square.


[[☃ image 1]]

This shape does not have four right angles, so it is not a square.

![A shape with four equal sides and two sets of congruent angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f3065ed7de93bcbd308e0cfe60cf729e.png)

This shape does not have four right angles or four sides of equal length, so it is not a square.

![A quadrilateral with two right angles and sides of unequal length.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ead185e540d92ce9cc5fc901b83b3aea.png)

Quadrilateral | Square/Not square 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles and four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4006a3393d1388fe9f3bf8028fdcb2db.png) | Square
![A shape with four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d999a4b9b555f28a412e0972f38dd14.png) | Not square
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d93227cd0e5b41a6c2e4571b554366eb.png)  | Not square

assessment_items.question_content:
All rectangles have four right angles and opposite sides that are equal in length and parallel.  

**Which of these quadrilaterals are rectangles?**

Quadrilateral | Rectangle/Not rectangle 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e8ea8f378807dde8c02f6cdeb87e241e.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa91818b3b5b6a304cb4451af9e537f9.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/beeb611f09e228055bcc0360f8f6d002.png)  | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints:
A rectangle is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four right angles. 

A *right angle*, or $90^\circ$ angle, is in the shape of a perfect corner, like the corner of a typical sheet of paper.

These shapes have four right angles, so they are rectangles.

![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef2daa7b2ea9bf560f19a2ceb9e06013771c11ce)![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23654e3de780321d5cfdd77e0fd06473d6116cc8)

This shape has only two right angles, so it is not a rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

Quadrilateral | Rectangle/Not rectangle 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e8ea8f378807dde8c02f6cdeb87e241e.png) | Rectangle
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa91818b3b5b6a304cb4451af9e537f9.png) | Rectangle
![A quadrilateral with two right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/beeb611f09e228055bcc0360f8f6d002.png)  | Not rectangle

assessment_items.question_content:
All rectangles have four right angles and opposite sides that are equal in length and parallel.  

**Which of these quadrilaterals are rectangles?**

Quadrilateral | Rectangle/Not rectangle 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with two sets of congruent angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7f04d18c2fd3b1256bb4f9465123e84a.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with two sets of congruent angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca83d2b24904d9ff548f3f02fb61643e.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68f9c4327157daf47de6b467ae8fea62.png)  | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints:
A rectangle is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four right angles. 

A right angle, or $90^\circ$ angle, is in the shape of a perfect corner, like the corner of a typical sheet of paper.

This shape has four right angles, so it is a rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

These shapes do not have four right angles, so they are not rectangles.



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

Quadrilateral | Rectangle/Not rectangle 
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with two sets of congruent angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7f04d18c2fd3b1256bb4f9465123e84a.png) | Not rectangle
![A quadrilateral with two sets of congruent angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca83d2b24904d9ff548f3f02fb61643e.png) | Not rectangle
![A quadrilateral with four right angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68f9c4327157daf47de6b467ae8fea62.png)  | Rectangle

assessment_items.question_content:
All rhombuses have all sides that are equal in length.  

**Which of these quadrilaterals are rhombuses?**

Quadrilateral | Rhombus/Not rhombus
:-: | :-:
![A four-sided figure. The bottom side is shorter than, but parallel to, the top side. The other sides are equal lengths, but not parallel.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/695526510299eb8e03146671c868efa7.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A parallelogram with 4 equal length sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d999a4b9b555f28a412e0972f38dd14.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A square with 4 right angles and 4 equal length sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2090df55fab2e71a72ee778dbc53f913.png)  | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints: A rhombus is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four sides of equal length.

These shapes have four sides of equal length, so they are rhombuses.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

This shape does not have four sides of equal length, so it is not a rhombus.

[[☃ image 1]]

Quadrilateral | Rhombus/Not rhombus
:-: | :-:
![A four-sided figure. The bottom side is shorter than, but parallel to, the top side. The other sides are equal lengths, but not parallel.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/695526510299eb8e03146671c868efa7.png) | Not rhombus
![A parallelogram with 4 equal length sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d999a4b9b555f28a412e0972f38dd14.png) | Rhombus
![A square with 4 right angles and 4 equal length sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2090df55fab2e71a72ee778dbc53f913.png)  | Rhombus

assessment_items.question_content:
All rhombuses have all sides that are equal in length and opposite sides that are parallel.  

**Which of these quadrilaterals are rhombuses?**

Quadrilateral | Rhombus/Not rhombus
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four equal sides and angles.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5398e431c3d7a7621c26ccb3d15af193.png) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A quadrilateral with four equal angles with opposite sides of equal length.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a23937d1aeaefb3b6a84c3056d7192d.png) | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
![A quadrilateral with four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb0c5a1899debb80fedc0947c6a854fa.png) | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints: A rhombus is a quadrilateral (four-sided shape) with four sides of equal length.

These shapes have four sides of equal length, so they are rhombuses.  


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

This shape does not have four sides of equal length, so it is not a rhombus.


[[☃ image 1]]

Quadrilateral | Rhombus/Not rhombus
:-: | :-:
![A quadrilateral with four equal angles and sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5398e431c3d7a7621c26ccb3d15af193.png) | Rhombus
![A quadrilateral with four equal angles with opposite sides of equal length.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a23937d1aeaefb3b6a84c3056d7192d.png) | Not rhombus
![A quadrilateral with four equal sides.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb0c5a1899debb80fedc0947c6a854fa.png) | Rhombus

assessment_items.question_content:
**What kinds of quadrilateral is the shape shown?**  
*The matching arrow labels indicate that two opposite sides are parallel.*

[[☃ explanation 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's look at each type of quadrilateral to see which ones can classify our shape.
##Parallelogram

A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with $2$ sets of parallel sides.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The shape is a quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides, so it is a parallelogram.


##Rhombus

A rhombus is a quadrilateral with all sides of equal length.

[[☃ explanation 3]]

The shape is a quadrilateral but it does **not** have $4$ sides of equal length, so it is **not** a rhombus.
###Rectangle

A rectangle is a quadrilateral with $4$ right angles.

[[☃ explanation 3]]

The shape is a quadrilateral, has $4$ right angles, and opposite sides are equal, so it is a rectangle.

###Square

A square is a quadrilateral with $4$ right angles and all sides of equal length.

[[☃ explanation 3]]

This shape is a quadrilateral but it does **not** have $4$ sides of equal length or $4$ right angles, so it is **not** a square.
The quadrilateral is a parallelogram and a rectangle.

options.explanation:
A $\purpleD{\text{parallelogram}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\purpleD{\text{2 sets of parallel sides}}$.

A $\blueD{\text{rectangle}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\blueD{\text{4 right angles}}$.

A $\greenD{\text{rhombus}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\greenD{\text{all sides of equal length}}$.

A $\goldD{\text{square}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\goldD{\text{4 right angles}}$ and $\goldD{\text{all sides of equal length}}$.

options.alt: A shape with four sides measuring 13, 7, 13, and 7. The shape has 4 right angles and 2 sets of parallel sides.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What kinds of quadrilateral is the shape shown?**

hints: Let's look at each type of quadrilateral to see which ones can classify our shape.

##Parallelogram

A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with $2$ sets of parallel sides.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The shape is a quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides, so it is a parallelogram.

##Rhombus

A rhombus is a quadrilateral with all sides of equal length.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The shape is a quadrilateral but it does **not** have $4$ sides of equal length, so it is **not** a rhombus.

###Rectangle

A rectangle is a quadrilateral with $4$ right angles.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The shape is a quadrilateral, has $4$ right angles, and opposite sides are equal, so it is a rectangle.

###Square

A square is a quadrilateral with $4$ right angles and all sides of equal length.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

This shape is a quadrilateral but it does **not** have $4$ sides of equal length or $4$ right angles, so it is **not** a square.
The quadrilateral is a parallelogram and a rectangle.

The quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

options.explanation:
A $\purpleD{\text{parallelogram}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\purpleD{\text{2 sets of parallel sides}}$.

A $\blueD{\text{rectangle}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\blueD{\text{4 right angles}}$.

A $\greenD{\text{rhombus}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\greenD{\text{all sides of equal length}}$.

A $\goldD{\text{square}}$ is a quadrilateral with $\goldD{\text{4 right angles}}$ and $\goldD{\text{all sides of equal length}}$.

options.alt: A shape with four sides measuring 5, 7, 5, and 7. The shape has 2 sets of parallel sides.
